# Formation Flight — Model Results

*Auto-generated by `scripts/generate_results.py` on 2026-05-31 12:17.*
*Re-run after model changes to update.*

## Structural Model

Power-law fit (empirical, calibrated to solar HALE data): `m_wing = 0.005812 × b^2.406`
- Data range: 25–78 m span
- Exponent 2.406 — superlinear scaling confirms structural penalty at large spans
- All wing-mass data points are estimated from published MTOW and typical structural fractions

The solver uses the **beam** structural model (`wingz/structures/beam.py`),
not this empirical fit. The empirical fit is shown here for reference and cross-check.

## Reference Comparisons

Max-payload results via `find_max_payload(N, span)`. Beam structural model, 30°N summer solstice, V formation 10% overlap.

| Config | N | AR | Chord(m) | Struct/ac(kg) | Payload/ac(W) | Batt/ac(kg) | AC mass(kg) | Fleet mass(kg) | Pld power(W) | Cost | $/kg pld |

| 1×60m | 1 | 14.0 | 4.29 | 84.6 | 1839.0 | 494.8 | 673.8 | 673.8 | 1839.0 | $26.9M | $293k |
| 2×40m | 2 | 11.7 | 3.43 | 43.5 | 1010.3 | 278.7 | 375.2 | 750.3 | 2020.6 | $15.8M | $157k |

| 4×20m | 4 | 8.3 | 2.40 | 14.7 | 265.7 | 88.3 | 118.7 | 474.8 | 1062.8 | $7.5M | $142k |
| 4×15m | 4 | 7.5 | 2.00 | 9.1 | 120.3 | 49.4 | 67.0 | 267.9 | 481.4 | $4.6M | $191k |
| 6×10m | 6 | 6.7 | 1.50 | 4.5 | 52.3 | 26.4 | 36.0 | 216.1 | 313.5 | $3.3M | $208k |
| 6×15m | 6 | 7.5 | 2.00 | 9.1 | 175.5 | 60.5 | 80.9 | 485.3 | 1052.8 | $6.5M | $124k |

## Position Strategy

Comparison for 4×20m formation (V, 10% overlap, 30°N summer solstice):

| Strategy | Description | Expected effect |
| --- | --- | --- |
| heavy_wake | Leader (lightest) up front; heavy nav aircraft in best wake slot | Max drag reduction for most power-hungry aircraft |
| heavy_front | Heavy aircraft leads (conventional) | Leader gets no wake benefit; baseline penalty |
| uniform | All aircraft identical mass/hardware | No position assignment needed; simpler control |

The solver does not expose position strategy directly — `find_max_payload` always uses the V-formation drag factors from `per_slot_drag_factor`. The qualitative effect is that `heavy_wake` maximises available payload by reducing the drag budget of the most power-limited aircraft. See `wingz/aerodynamics/formation_aero.py` and `wingz/evaluation/solver.py` for implementation.

## Model Assumptions

| Parameter | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stall margin (day) | 1.15 | V_cruise / V_stall |
| Stall margin (night) | 1.03 | Near-stall in calm stratosphere |
| Panel efficiency | 38% | MicroLink III-V ELO, flight-proven on Zephyr/PHASA-35 |
| Panel coverage | 80% | Upper wing, excl. ailerons/spar caps/root |
| Propulsion efficiency | 75% | Motor × propeller × ESC |
| Battery density | 250 Wh/kg | Cell-level Li-ion; aerospace pack ~10% lower |
| CD0 | 0.025 | Zero-lift drag (wing only) |
| Oswald e | 0.85 | Span efficiency factor |
| Latitude | 30.0°N | |
| Day of year | 172 | Summer solstice (~21 Jun) |
| Structural model | Beam | `wingz/structures/beam.py` |
| Dawn SOC | 0 | Battery empty at dawn (conservative sizing) |
| Station-keeping | 4 servos × 2.5W, base duty 0.25 | ~3.8W/follower light turbulence |

## Caveats

- Wing mass data is all estimated (derived from published MTOW and typical structural fractions)
- Formation drag uses V-formation classical wake model; no vortex lattice or CFD
- Cost model uses 2024-2026 aerospace market data; will evolve as better data arrives
- Solar irradiance uses simplified analytical model (not full radiative transfer)
- No dynamic effects modeled: gusts, control latency, formation break-up/rejoin
- Position strategy (heavy_wake vs heavy_front) is not yet exposed as a sweep parameter in the solver
- Battery density is cell-level; pack-level is ~10% lower (BMS, wiring, enclosure)
